page: Microsoft Azure D8als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dalv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz. The system is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not bundle any local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage for external disk needs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 10% of tested hardware. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench single-core metrics also show strong performance, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall into the average tier. Memory bandwidth for large, potentially uncached blocks is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%, while small-block cached reads and large-block writes maintain average throughput.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high single-core compute performance, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It is well-suited for tasks such as text processing, web serving, single-threaded data decompression, and lightweight software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure D8als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, paired with 16.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and text processing benchmarks ranking in the top 10%. Conversely, uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, placing in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, low-memory workloads. It is suited for single-threaded processing, text manipulation, web serving, and light compilation tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8als_v6 is an 8-vCPU compute-optimized virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz, 16 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for text and data processing.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8als_v6 is a compute-optimized VM with 8 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 3.7 GHz CPU, and 16 GB RAM, optimized for single-core workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processors and dedicated vCPUs for single-core intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Configured with 16.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per vCPU)
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance
No local storage included, but supports Premium Storage
Best suited for web serving, text processing, and single-threaded tasks
